Definition: Directive to cease action/attack/event/mission.
Orbit about a specific point; refueling track flown by tanker.
Height of friendly aircraft in thousands of feet mean sea level (MSL).
FRIENDLY command and control agency when call sign is not known.
No ARM ordnance remaining.
Unit or element will be performing exactly as stated by the air tasking order.
To request or provide a response to a coded challenge.
Any communications relay using automatic retransmissions.
Release/launch of specified weapon.
A picture label describing two groups separated laterally.
Informative call that the displayed map is unusable due to contrast/resolution/terrain masking, etc.
Positively identified as an enemy IAW theater identification criteria. The term does not imply direction or authority to engage.
Execute(ing) launch and decide tactics with the intent to maneuver into the visual arena.
Reference number used to indicate such information as altitude, fuel, or distance from a reference point.
FRIENDLY net-enabled glide weapon(s) with weapon-to-weapon communication capability (e.g., SDB II).
Carry out deception plan indicated or IAW previous orders.
Last transmission potentially disclosed unauthorized information.
CONTACT stabilized within 60-109 degrees of aspect.
Potential JAMMER along the stated azimuth/direction.
System indicated is inoperative. Cancelled by SWEET.
Aircraft is out of or unable to employ long-range precision guided munitions.
FRIENDLY long-range surface-to-air missile (SAM).
Prebriefed fuel state needed for recovery.
FRIENDLY surface-to-air missile(s). Modifiers include: AWAY, DEFEATED, ENGAGED.
Call to configure sensors IAW mission-specific instructions (e.g., for target tracking).
Notification of potential for blue-on-blue (friendly fire) situation.
Directive to turn off all external aircraft lighting.
No visual contact with FRIENDLY aircraft/ground position; opposite of VISUAL.
Directive or informative call that indicates aircraft will continue straight ahead at the merge and not become anchored with target/targets.
A radar or visual air contact whose identity is unknown.
Tactical control format providing Bearing, Range, Altitude, and Aspect relative to the specified FRIENDLY aircraft.
FRIENDLY air-launched anti-ship missile.
Radar locked to a known FRIENDLY aircraft. Response to a SPIKE or BUDDY SPIKE call.
FRIENDLY aircraft air-to-air radar track indication on radar warning receiver (RWR).
An established reference point from which the position of an object can be referenced by bearing (magnetic) and range (nm).
Directive to fly at maximum continuous speed (military power).
Directive call to establish a combat air patrol at a specified point.
Descriptive term for aircraft in a CAP.
Aircrew has acquired and is able to track a specified ground target with an onboard sensor.
Active missile data link terminated between high and medium pulse repetition frequency active.
No sensor information on a GROUP of interest.
Ordnance release is authorized. Typically used in CAS.
Used by JTAC/FAC(A) to grant weapons release authority in Type 3 control.
Directive/informative call to switch from normal/overt external lighting to covert NVD-only lighting.
Decreasing in separation.
Initiate turn in the CAP away from the anticipated threats.
Request for information on a system at stated location; implies a request for ambiguity resolution.
Directive call to intercept a GROUP of interest.
Sensor contact at the stated position.
Continue present maneuver; does not imply a change in clearance to engage or expend ordnance.
Return to cruise speed after BUSTER or GATE.
Directive or informative call indicating a loss of all engine power.
Aircraft is in a defensive maneuver and reacting to a threat.
Request for additional target information/description.
CONTACT stabilized at 0-60 degrees aspect.
Two weapons employed against the same target.
Directive to change altitude to the stated level.
Sensor data is lost on GROUP or CONTACT.
CONTACT stabilized at 120-150 degrees aspect.
Directive to turn on IR floodlight or searchlight.
Simulated or actual launch of air-to-air weapons (1=Semi-active, 2=IR, 3=Active).
Radar or emitter equipment.
Fly as fast as possible, using reheat/afterburner.
Sensor target is approaching azimuth or elevation tracking limits.
Go to briefed radio frequency/channel using hopping/secure crypto.
Momentary radar return(s).
Turn in the CAP toward the anticipated threat.
Active radar missile is at high pulse repetition frequency (HPRF) active range.
Ground-based laser designator is on.
Fuel status above BINGO at which separation or termination should begin.
An aircraft in a minor state of emergency.
Air-to-ground ordnance release without specific coordination (emergency/tactical).
Directive to turn on/off all external lights.
Fly at speed giving maximum cruising range.
FRIENDLY long-range air-to-surface missile (e.g., AGM-130).
A request for the tactical ground picture in an area of interest.
Directive to join formation.
Bias radar coverage and scan volume to gain situation awareness on the assigned GROUP.
HAVE QUICK time-of-day (TOD) signal.
RWR ground threat indication with no launch indication.
RWR indication of AI radar in search.
No RWR indications.
Active radar missile is at medium pulse repetition frequency (MPRF) active range.
No air-to-air or air-to-surface ordnance remaining except for self-protection.
FRIENDLY air-to-surface missile launch.
Timeline adjustment in minutes; always a delay from planned time.
FRIENDLY net-enabled weapon.

One weave; a single crossing of flight paths.
Execute(ing) launch and leave tactics.
Perform high-G descending turn in the stated direction.
Clearance to employ ordnance/fires on surface target coordinates.
Aircraft is using a targeting pod.
Assignment of responsibility within a GROUP.
Equipment indicated is operating inefficiently (opposite of SWEET).
RWR indication of an AI threat in track or launch.
Target destroyed.
Directive to enter a tactical spread formation.
Radar indication(s) of noise jamming.
Equipment indicated is operating efficiently.
Enough fuel and ordnance to accept a commitment.
Shooter assesses valid BVR shot parameters have been met and missile has reached termination.
FRIENDLY missile defeated.
